title: Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal
description: Veterinary Nurse – Small Animal All Care Veterinary Centre, Walkinstown, Dublin 12 Full\-time, Permanent Salary €30,000\-€40,000 DOE Grow Your Nursing Career in a Supportive, Friendly Practice Whether you’re a newly qualified Veterinary Nurse finding your feet, or a more experienced nurse ready to settle into a positive, well\-run practice, All Care Veterinary Centre offers a place where you can feel supported, valued, and confident in your role. We’re a well\-established 100% small animal practice in Walkinstown, known for our loyal clients, high standards of care, and genuinely welcoming team. This role is ideal for a junior to mid\-level RVN who enjoys variety, teamwork, and the chance to keep developing without the pressure of out\-of\-hours or on\-call work. A Role Designed Around Balance \& Progression This is a permanent position, with full\-time preferred, though part\-time hours can be considered for the right candidate. A full\-time role is based on a 40\-hour week, with shifts spread across Monday to Saturday. Typical shifts are 8am–4pm or 11am–7pm, offering flexibility across the week. Weekend work is shared fairly, with up to two Saturdays per month (8am–3pm). There’s no out\-of\-hours, no Sundays, and no bank holiday work, allowing you to enjoy a healthy and sustainable work–life balance. Your Day\-to\-Day \- Varied, Hands\-on \& Supported As part of the nursing team at All Care, you’ll be involved across all areas of small animal nursing, including surgical nursing, medical and clinical nursing, inpatient care, dentistry, radiography, laboratory work, infection control, dispensary management, and client communication. You’ll be trusted with real responsibility while always having experienced colleagues around you for support. Whether you’re still building confidence or looking to broaden your skillset, this is a role where you can develop at your own pace and feel genuinely part of the practice. A Modern Practice With a Great Team Culture All Care Veterinary Centre is fully equipped with digital x\-ray, ultrasound, and dental facilities, allowing you to work to a high clinical standard. Our team includes 3 Veterinary Surgeons, 4 Registered Veterinary Nurses, a student nurse, practice manager, and receptionist, creating a close\-knit and supportive environment where everyone plays a key role. We pride ourselves on our positive atmosphere, strong communication, and shared commitment to excellent patient care and client service. Who We’re Looking For We’re open to all levels of experience, from newly qualified nurses to those with several years in practice. You must be VCI registered or eligible to register. What matters most to us is attitude \- someone who is: * A team player with a positive outlook * Caring, organised and motivated * Keen to learn, develop and deliver great patient care Graduate nurses will have access to our Graduate Nurse Academy, while more experienced nurses can explore further development through Nurse Evolve, leadership pathways and funded certificates. Benefits – subject to pro rata We Recognise That Our People Are Fundamental To The Success Of Our Business. Investing In Our Staff, Premises And Processes Is At The Heart Of What We Do. title: Warehouse Operative
description: · Unloading trucks/trailers on the loading bay. · Unloading trucks/trailers in the yard. · Checking and labelling of all goods inward materials as directed by the supervisor. · Inspect materials received for storage or being pick for dispatch as directed by the supervisor. · Putting away all inbound pallets as directed by the supervisor. · Cleaning of allocated area, removing all broken or unwanted pallets, removal of all loose plastics. · Sweep and remove all debris from the isles. · Report any damages to products or equipment to your supervisor or manager asap. · Inform details of any non\-conformances noted in the course of the work. · Operate a clean as you go policy while carrying out your duties. · Stock checking as requested by the supervisor. · Pick \& pack orders as directed by the supervisor. · Ensure that goods are sufficiently protected and secure, as required, prior to dispatch. · Take care when loading and unloading pallets to ensure that damages are avoided. **Work as part of the warehouse team and move between warehouse as requested by the warehouse manager.** Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €15\.00\-€16\.00 per hour Education: * Junior Certificate (preferred) Experience: * VNA forklift Driver: 1 year (required) Licence/Certification: * Forklift license counter balance (required) Work Location: In person

title: Warehouse Operative (9am-5pm)
description: **The Opportunity:** -------------------- **Location:** Orion Business Campus, Northwest Business Park, Ballycoolin, Blanchardstown, Dublin 15, Ireland **Working Hours:** Monday to Friday, 9:00am – 5:00pm ### **Job Purpose** The Warehouse Operative supports the efficient day\-to\-day operation of the warehouse by accurately picking, packing, handling, and delivering goods while maintaining high standards of safety, quality, productivity, and customer service. ### **Key Responsibilities** * Accurately pick and pack products in line with order requirements * Handle batch\-controlled and expiry\-dated products, ensuring correct stock rotation (FIFO / FEFO) * Load and unload trailers and delivery vehicles safely and efficiently * Transport and deliver goods in accordance with schedules provided by the dispatcher * Secure loads correctly and ensure weight limits are adhered to * Verify the quantity and condition of goods during loading and unloading * Report shortages, overages, damage, or discrepancies promptly * Operate truck/van units and material handling equipment safely * Report vehicle defects or maintenance issues to the appropriate department * Carry out general warehouse duties, including material movement, equipment operation, and record keeping * Assist with inbound and outbound shipments as required * Maintain a clean, organised, and safe working environment * Build positive working relationships by acting in a professional and courteous manner * Perform other duties as reasonably required ### **Health, Safety \& Compliance** * Comply with all warehouse health and safety rules and site\-specific procedures * Use provided Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) correctly at all times * Follow safe manual handling and equipment operation practices * Participate in mandatory training and refresher sessions * Support a positive safety culture by identifying and reporting hazards or concerns ### **Skills \& Experience** * Previous warehouse or logistics experience desirable (but not essential) * Minimum of one year’s relevant experience preferred * Leaving Certificate or equivalent qualification * Strong attention to detail, particularly when handling batch\-controlled or expiry\-dated products * Ability to meet targets in a fast\-paced environment * Good communication and teamwork skills * Physically capable of manual handling tasks ### **Licences \& Equipment** * Valid truck or van driving licence required * Experience using material handling equipment (pallet jacks, forklifts) is required ### **Personal Attributes** * Reliable and punctual * Safety\-conscious and process\-driven * Professional, respectful, and cooperative * Willing to learn and follow instructions ### **Working Environment \& Physical Requirements** * Industrial warehouse environment with exposure to noise, machinery, temperature changes, dust, fumes, or gases * Work may involve operating vehicles and equipment and working at height * Physical duties include lifting, carrying, pushing, or pulling up to 75 lbs with or without assistance * Regular bending, climbing, kneeling, twisting, standing, and repetitive movements * Ability to sit for a minimum of two hours continuously * Vision requirements include close vision, depth perception, peripheral vision, and the ability to adjust focus ### **What We Offer** * Full\-time, daytime working hours (Monday to Friday) * A supportive and safety\-focused team environment * Stable employment within a well\-established operation **Disclaimer:** The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this classification. They are not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required of employees assigned to this position. title: Warehouse Operative (Velux)
description: **General information** ----------------------- ### **Legal entity** GEODIS is a leading global logistics provider acknowledged for its expertise across all aspects of the supply chain. As a growth partner to its clients, GEODIS specializes in five lines of business: Supply Chain Optimization, Global Freight Forwarding, Global Contract Logistics, Distribution \& Express, and European Road Network. With a global network spanning nearly 166 countries and more than 49, 720 employees, GEODIS is ranked no. 6 in its sector across the world. In 2024, GEODIS generated €11\.3 billion in revenue. GEODIS is a company owned by SNCF Group. This position is also open to anyone recognized as a disabled worker. ### **Reference** 2026\-17157 ### **Line Of Business** FREIGHT MANAGEMENT **Position description** ------------------------ ### **Type of assignment** Full\-time Regular ### **Function** Operational activities \- Freight Administration ### **Job title** Warehouse Operative (Velux) ### **Job description** Loading and Unloading * Assist with the loading and unloading of goods at multiple dock doors, ensuring efficient flow of products in and out of the warehouse. * Safely handle trucks and vehicles, supporting transport and logistics operations. Warehouse Management * Support the management of goods within the warehouse, including receiving, storing, and preparing items for delivery. * Maintain accurate inventory records and ensure proper storage conditions to optimize inventory turnover and reduce storage costs. * Up to date with Customs Regulations * Health and Safety policies and legislation that impacts this role Order Fulfilment * Prepare goods for delivery * Ensure timely and accurate picking, packing, and dispatch of orders, supporting contract logistics operations. Operational Support * Contribute to the operation of a large logistics warehouse facility and distribution center. * Assist in the implementation of innovative logistics solutions aimed at optimising flow management and improving efficiency. Sustainability and Safety * Follow all safety protocols and guidelines, prioritising working conditions and safety for self and colleagues. Ethical Conduct * Respect clients and subcontractors, fostering strong relationships based on mutual respect and trust. * Uphold GEODIS's values of sustainability, innovation, and ethical behavior in daily work. ### **Applicant's profile** * Passionate and Dedicated: Demonstrates passion and excellence in logistics operations, supporting the prosperity of clients and the company. * Adaptable: Able to respond to changing demands and contemporary challenges in the logistics environment. * Team\-Oriented: Works collaboratively within the warehouse team, fostering strong relationships based on mutual respect and trust. * Operational Awareness: Understands and supports loading/unloading operations at multiple dock doors, managing vehicles and goods. * Warehouse Management: Capable of receiving, storing, and preparing items for delivery, maintaining accurate inventory records, and optimizing inventory turnover. * Order Fulfilment: Able to pick, pack, and dispatch orders efficiently and accurately, contributing to express and contract logistics operations. * Safety\-Conscious: Prioritizes working conditions and safety, following all protocols and guidelines to ensure a safe workplace for self and colleagues. * Ethical Conduct: Upholds GEODIS's values of sustainability, innovation, and ethical behavior in daily work, respecting clients and subcontractors. * Computer: Fully PC literate on excel and Word applications * Customer service skills: High standard Education * PPT License essential * Man Up License highly desirable * Manual Handling essential * Reach Truck License desirable * Counterbalance License desirable * HPRA experience desirable * Retail experience within a warehouse environment essential ### **Working time (%)** 100 **Position location** --------------------- ### **Position place** Europe, Ireland, Leinster (Laighin), Dublin (Baile Átha Cliath) ### **Location** Geodis Dublin Airport Logistics Park, St Margarets, Dublin 11, Ireland **Candidate criteria** ---------------------- ### **Minimum level of education required** HIGH/SECONDARY SCHOOL DEGREE ### **Years of experience in similar position** Junior (1 to 5 years of experience)
